title: Creating a Security Agent with Cohere North and Wiz | Cohere
gist: Cohere built a security incident response agent by connecting its North enterprise AI platform to Wiz via a custom MCP server, automating triage workflows that previously took 30 minutes to 2 hours per finding. The architecture exposes eight atomic Wiz tools through a lightweight Python server, enabling North to handle toxic combination analysis, IR report drafting, ticket creation, and status updates from a single prompt. The integration removed the first-pass triage loop entirely, allowing security engineers to start from pre-populated IR reports rather than raw alerts.

insights.claim: Cohere's manual cloud security triage process consumed 30 minutes to 2 hours per critical finding, a pace unsustainable given rapid cloud footprint growth, making automation the only viable scaling path without headcount increases.
insights.speaker: Cohere
insights.so_what: Security automation has a measurable ROI anchor: each automated triage event saves up to 2 hours of senior engineer time, compounding across a growing cloud estate.
insights.quote: That process could take 30 minutes to 2 hours per finding. Not because the signal was unclear, but because the path from insight to action wasn't yet built for our exact environment, tools, and team rhythm.

insights.claim: Cohere's North-Wiz integration shifted the first human touchpoint in security response from reading raw alerts to evaluating an agent's pre-populated assessment, fundamentally changing how security engineers allocate cognitive effort.
insights.speaker: Cohere
insights.so_what: AI agents that shift human-in-the-loop to the evaluation stage rather than the discovery stage multiply engineer effectiveness without increasing headcount.
insights.quote: For critical findings, the first human touchpoint shifted from reading the alert to evaluating the agent's assessment — a smarter, more strategic use of human expertise.

insights.claim: A 20-second AI blast radius analysis of Wiz toxic combination findings replaces what previously consumed half of a security engineer's morning, representing a 30x+ time compression on the most cognitively demanding security triage task.
insights.speaker: Cohere
insights.so_what: Enterprise AI agents targeting high-skill knowledge work bottlenecks (not simple data entry) deliver the largest measurable productivity gains.
insights.quote: This 20-second analysis replaces what previously consumed half of a security engineer's morning.

themes.theme: MCP as Enterprise AI Integration Standard

insights.claim: Cohere built its North-Wiz security integration using Model Context Protocol (MCP), enabling a clean architecture where a single Python MCP server exposes eight atomic Wiz tools discoverable by North without bespoke API integration work.
insights.speaker: Cohere
insights.so_what: MCP is becoming the enterprise API layer for AI agents — companies investing in MCP-native platforms gain integration leverage that compounds across workflows.
insights.quote: North natively speaks MCP, enabling a clean, extensible architecture: North (agent platform) → Custom MCP server → Wiz GraphQL API

insights.claim: North's MCP-native architecture allows a single Wiz integration to be built once and exposed across all security workflows, providing integration leverage that would be unavailable with point-to-point API connections.
insights.speaker: Cohere
insights.so_what: Platform-level MCP adoption creates a compounding moat: each new integration becomes immediately available to all existing and future workflows.
insights.quote: North's MCP-native architecture provided the leverage we needed: build the Wiz integration once and expose it across all workflows.

insights.claim: The North-Wiz MCP server uses a shared secret header for North authentication and OAuth2 client credentials for Wiz, keeping service account tokens secure server-side — a security pattern critical for regulated industry deployments.
insights.speaker: Cohere
insights.so_what: For enterprise AI agents in regulated environments, the authentication architecture of MCP servers is as important as the functional capabilities — a differentiator for platforms that get it right.
insights.quote: North authenticates to the MCP server via a shared secret header, while the server uses OAuth2 client credentials for Wiz, keeping service account tokens secure and server-side.

insights.claim: Wiz identifies 'toxic combinations' — multi-factor attack paths where individually manageable risks chain into critical exposures — and North automates ranked blast radius analysis of these chains weighing internet exposure, privilege level, and data sensitivity.
insights.speaker: Cohere
insights.so_what: Cloud security vendors offering compound risk scoring (not just individual CVEs) are addressing the highest-value triage problem; AI agents that reason over attack chains amplify that value.
insights.quote: Wiz surfaces the toxic combinations of risk factors that create critical attack paths, such as an internet-facing VM with a critical vulnerability and high-privilege IAM access.

insights.claim: North uses Wiz's attackChain.description field containing narrative multi-hop attack path descriptions as input for its reasoning engine to produce prioritized risk tables, demonstrating how LLM reasoning adds value over raw API data.
insights.speaker: Cohere
insights.so_what: Security platforms with rich narrative attack chain descriptions (not just metadata) become significantly more valuable when paired with reasoning-capable AI agents.
insights.quote: reads the attackChain.description field on each finding (which contains Wiz's narrative of the multi-hop attack path), then uses Reasoning to rank them.

insights.claim: Cohere deployed a scheduled North automation that runs every Monday at 3:00 a.m. to generate a security posture document covering aggregated metrics, toxic combinations, and CISA KEV CVEs — delivering consistent visibility without any manual analyst effort.
insights.speaker: Cohere
insights.so_what: Scheduled AI agent workflows that replace recurring manual reporting eliminate both the labor cost and the human inconsistency in what gets measured and flagged week-to-week.
insights.quote: We built a North automation — a scheduled graph-based workflow — that runs every Monday at 3:00 a.m. and produces a security posture document without anyone asking for it.

insights.claim: The autonomous weekly brief surfaces CISA Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) present in Cohere's infrastructure as a standing agenda item, ensuring actively exploited CVEs never get missed in the weekly security cycle regardless of analyst workload.
insights.speaker: Cohere
insights.so_what: AI security workflows incorporating threat intelligence feeds (CISA KEV) alongside internal inventory data represent a higher-value integration pattern than single-source automation.
insights.quote: wiz_search_vulnerabilities(has_cisa_kev=True) for actively exploited CVEs in our environment

themes.theme: AI Agent Hallucination Prevention in High-Stakes Contexts

insights.claim: Cohere encoded strict IR report formats in system prompts — including rules preventing the agent from inferring asset names — after hitting hallucination problems in early iterations, establishing that prompt-level constraints are necessary for safety in security automation.
insights.speaker: Cohere
insights.so_what: Enterprise AI deployment in security contexts requires explicit hallucination guards encoded at the system prompt level, not just post-hoc output validation — a design lesson with broad applicability.
insights.quote: The report format is strict, encoded in the system prompt to prevent the hallucination problems we hit in early iterations

insights.claim: North maintains strict data fidelity by using exact Wiz field values rather than inferring names, a design constraint that separates security-grade AI agents from general-purpose ones and is key to adoption in regulated industries.
insights.speaker: Cohere
insights.so_what: AI agent platforms that enforce data fidelity (no inferred values) rather than merely generating plausible text have a structural advantage in security and compliance use cases.
insights.quote: The platform's reasoning capabilities handle complex security analysis while maintaining strict data fidelity, using exact Wiz field values rather than inferring names.

insights.claim: Cohere distinguishes finding types (configuration vs. vulnerability) within its system prompt to limit hallucinations via context-aware enrichment, reflecting that generic prompting is insufficient for reliable security AI agents.
insights.speaker: Cohere
insights.so_what: Security AI agents require domain-specific prompt engineering that differentiates finding types, not one-size-fits-all instructions — a barrier to entry protecting specialized vendors.
insights.quote: Context-aware enrichment: Limit hallucinations by distinguishing finding types (configuration vs. vulnerability).

themes.theme: North Platform Positioning as Security Operations Hub

insights.claim: Cohere positions North as 'the central nervous system for modern security operations,' claiming that MCP-native architecture plus reasoning capabilities uniquely qualifies it over generic agent platforms for security use cases.
insights.speaker: Cohere
insights.so_what: Cohere is making a direct bid for enterprise security operations budgets, competing with SOAR vendors and general-purpose AI platforms on the argument that MCP-nativeness and reasoning depth are differentiating.
insights.quote: This combination of integration flexibility and analytical precision makes North uniquely positioned as the central nervous system for modern security operations.

insights.claim: Cohere targets 'regulated industries and governments' as its primary North market and emphasizes 'secure, sovereign AI' — indicating a deliberate go-to-market strategy around compliance-sensitive buyers rather than cost-optimizing commercial buyers.
insights.speaker: Cohere
insights.so_what: Cohere is positioning North away from head-to-head competition with OpenAI/Microsoft Copilot by focusing on sovereign/regulated markets where data residency and compliance constraints limit hyperscaler alternatives.
insights.quote: At Cohere, we build secure, sovereign AI for mission-critical environments across regulated industries and governments.

insights.claim: Cohere open-sourced the full North-Wiz security integration in a public repository (cohere-ai/cohere-security-toolkit), using open-source distribution as a go-to-market strategy to accelerate enterprise adoption of the North platform.
insights.speaker: Cohere
insights.so_what: Publishing reference implementations as open source lowers enterprise evaluation friction and accelerates platform-level sales — a classic developer-led go-to-market strategy for B2B AI infrastructure companies.
insights.quote: The full implementation is available in our public repository, enabling your organization to replicate this pattern and transform your security response capabilities.

themes.theme: Technical Implementation Lessons for Enterprise AI Agents

insights.claim: Cohere's implementation established that prompt placement matters more than prompt length — critical instructions must appear at the top of system prompts rather than embedded mid-text — a generalizable engineering lesson for production AI agents.
insights.speaker: Cohere
insights.so_what: Prompt engineering best practices for production agents differ from research settings; position-sensitive instruction placement is a key operational variable that affects output reliability at scale.
insights.quote: Prompt placement > length: Ensure that critical instructions appear at the top of system prompts.

insights.claim: Cohere's team found that building MCP tools around actual API behavior rather than documentation prevented integration failures, reflecting a broader risk in AI agent development where LLMs may hallucinate API capabilities.
insights.speaker: Cohere
insights.so_what: Production AI agent deployments require empirical API validation rather than spec-driven development — teams that skip this step face silent failures in production that are hard to debug.
insights.quote: Validate API assumptions: Build tools around actual API behavior, not documentation.

insights.claim: The architecture exposes Wiz's capabilities as eight atomic, single-purpose MCP tools (list, get, search, query, update) rather than compound tools, following a design principle that atomic tools compose more reliably than monolithic integrations.
insights.speaker: Cohere
insights.so_what: Atomic tool design in MCP servers enables agent reasoning to compose capabilities flexibly — enterprise AI platform vendors should document this pattern as an architectural standard.
insights.quote: The lightweight Python MCP server exposes Wiz's capabilities as eight atomic tools
